behaviors.description: =SUM(A1:A10) evaluates to the correct sum of cells A1 through A10
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This tests a single formula evaluation path — range parsing, cell value lookup, and arithmetic — all within the formula engine object. The failure mode is algorithmic (wrong sum, wrong range parsing), not a module-boundary handoff. REAL-INFRA: No OS/network needed. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: No. A unit test can construct an in-memory cell grid, assign values to A1:A10, and assert the evaluated result. REJECTION-GATE: Integration would require a separate module boundary to be crossed — but the behavior is about the formula evaluator's internal logic, not a handoff between distinct subsystems.
behaviors.test_description: Construct an in-memory cell grid with numeric values in A1:A10, set a formula cell to =SUM(A1:A10), invoke evaluate(), and assert the result equals the expected sum. Also test edge cases: all zeros, single cell, mixed positive/negative.

behaviors.description: A formula referencing another formula cell (=B1+1 where B1=A1*2) produces the correct cascaded result
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure here only surfaces when the formula parser, cell reference resolver, and evaluator cooperate — specifically when the evaluator must recursively resolve an intermediate formula cell before completing its own computation. This is a module-boundary handoff (reference resolver hands formula cells back to the evaluator). A unit test of any single module in isolation cannot reproduce this. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No OS/network. (b) No AI/ML. (c) No multi-agent. Stays at Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire together the formula parser, cell graph, and evaluator. Create cells: A1=5, B1==A1*2, C1==B1+1. Call evaluate on C1 and assert it returns 11. Vary A1 and verify C1 updates correctly through the chain.

behaviors.description: Circular dependency (A1 refs B1, B1 refs A1) is detected and returns a circular reference error instead of looping
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Cycle detection is a graph algorithm applied to the dependency graph — a single, self-contained data structure. The failure mode (infinite loop or stack overflow) appears within the cycle-detection logic itself, not at a boundary between distinct modules. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No. REJECTION-GATE: Integration would require this to fail only when two modules hand off state — but the cycle detector operates on the dependency graph as a unit.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a dependency graph with A1 depending on B1 and B1 depending on A1. Invoke the cycle detection or evaluation entry point and assert the result is a CircularReferenceError (or equivalent), not a hang or exception. Test a 3-node cycle as well.

behaviors.description: VLOOKUP exact match returns correct value; returns error when key is not found
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: VLOOKUP is a built-in function whose logic — scan a table range for a matching key, return the Nth column — is self-contained within the function implementation. Both the match path and the not-found path are algorithmic branches inside one function. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No. REJECTION-GATE: No distinct module boundary is crossed; the lookup table is an in-memory cell range passed to the function.
behaviors.test_description: Build an in-memory lookup table (e.g., a 4x2 cell range with keys and values). Call VLOOKUP with a key that exists and assert the correct column value is returned. Call VLOOKUP with a missing key and assert the expected error (#N/A or equivalent) is returned. Test boundary: key at first and last row.

behaviors.description: IF correctly handles nested conditions (=IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, 'high', 'medium'), 'low'))
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: Nested IF evaluation is pure expression tree traversal within the formula evaluator — the failure mode (wrong branch taken, wrong result) is algorithmic, not a module-boundary issue. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No. REJECTION-GATE: No distinct module boundary is crossed; the entire IF tree is evaluated by the expression evaluator alone.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1 to each of three representative values (e.g., 5, 15, 25) and evaluate =IF(A1>10, IF(A1>20, 'high', 'medium'), 'low'). Assert the results are 'low', 'medium', and 'high' respectively. Also test boundary values (10, 20) for edge-case branch selection.

behaviors.description: CONCATENATE joins cell values with correct separator and skips empty cells rather than inserting blank strings
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The behavior describes two specific algorithmic rules inside the CONCATENATE function: separator insertion and empty-cell filtering. Both are implementable and testable as internal function logic. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No. REJECTION-GATE: No module boundary is required; the function takes a list of resolved cell values and applies string operations.
behaviors.test_description: Call CONCATENATE with a mix of non-empty and empty cell values. Assert that (a) non-empty values are joined with the correct separator, and (b) empty cells produce no blank entry in the output (the separator does not appear for them). Test all-empty case, single-value case, and adjacently empty cells.

behaviors.description: A cross-sheet reference (=Sheet2!A1+1) resolves correctly when the referenced sheet exists
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure mode only surfaces at the boundary between the formula evaluator and the workbook/sheet manager: the evaluator must request a cell from a *different sheet object*, which requires the sheet registry to locate Sheet2 and return its cell. A unit test of the evaluator alone with a stubbed sheet lookup would not reveal bugs in the actual sheet dispatch path. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No OS. (b) No AI. (c) No multi-agent. Stays at Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create a workbook with two in-memory sheets. Set Sheet2!A1 = 10. On Sheet1, evaluate =Sheet2!A1+1 and assert the result is 11. Also test that referencing a non-existent sheet returns the appropriate #REF! error.

behaviors.description: When a referenced cell's value changes, all dependent formulas recalculate in correct topological order
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure — wrong order of recalculation, stale intermediate values — only emerges when the dependency tracker, topological sorter, and formula evaluator cooperate on a live cell change event. No single module in isolation can exhibit this bug; it requires the change-notification path to wire through all three subsystems. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No OS. (b) No AI. (c) No multi-agent. Stays at Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Build a dependency chain: A1 (value), B1==A1*2, C1==B1+3. Change A1 and trigger recalculation. Assert B1 and C1 are updated in dependency order and hold correct values. Add a diamond dependency (D1==A1+B1) to verify the topological sort handles a shared ancestor correctly.

behaviors.description: Undo after editing a formula restores both the formula text and all previously computed dependent values
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure requires the undo stack, formula evaluator, and dependency recalculation subsystem to interact: after undo the engine must restore the formula text *and* re-evaluate all downstream cells to their prior results. A bug in any one of these modules' interaction (e.g., undo restores formula text but triggers a stale recalculation) only appears when all three are wired together. REAL-INFRA: No. LLM-DEP: No. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) No OS. (b) No AI. (c) No multi-agent. Stays at Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Set A1=5, B1==A1*2 (evaluates to 10). Edit B1's formula to ==A1+100 (evaluates to 105). Call undo(). Assert B1's formula text is restored to ==A1*2 and its computed value is 10. Verify a dependent of B1 (e.g., C1==B1+1) also returns to its prior value of 11.

behaviors.description: Saving and loading a workbook produces identical cell values and formulas with no data loss or floating-point precision change
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure (data loss, precision change) is in the serialization/deserialization pipeline — multiple modules (serializer, binary formatter, deserializer, cell reconstruction) must cooperate. REAL-INFRA: The core precision and data-loss bugs are in the binary encoding logic, not in OS-level file behavior; a tempfile or BytesIO write/read cycle reproduces the exact failure mode without real filesystem I/O. An in-process round-trip test using BytesIO or a tempfile is sufficient. REJECTION-GATE for System: the behavior's failure mode is not about OS buffering, permissions, or real file handle lifecycle — it is about the correctness of the binary format. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) Tempfile suffices — no real OS behavior needed. (b) No AI. (c) No multi-agent. Stays at Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Build a workbook with cells containing integer values, floating-point values (with many decimal places), formula strings, and empty cells. Serialize to a BytesIO buffer (or tempfile). Deserialize a new workbook from that buffer. Assert each cell's formula text and computed value are bit-for-bit identical to the originals. Include a cell with a value like 1/3 to verify floating-point precision is preserved.
